title: Outreach Memo
subtitle: A concise University of Iowa summary of the full opportunity
summary: A short University of Iowa-facing routing memo for internal forwarding, summarizing the opportunity and directing readers to the right next conversation.
synopsis: A concise bridge for faculty, grants staff, development staff, and other internal stakeholders who need the opportunity explained quickly without being pulled through every supporting argument. It states the immediate ask, gives the shortest useful summary of Steve Slevinski's present-tense value, and then routes readers toward PI, operational, infrastructure, or development conversations without collapsing those lanes together.

slides.title: University of Iowa Outreach Memo
slides.summary: The shortest version of the case is simple: route the Iowa conversation clearly between lab placement, practical DMS value, and the separate long-term infrastructure opportunity.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for the outreach memo overview.
slides.speech: The University of Iowa opportunity can be stated simply. One conversation is about a lab home under a PI. Another is about practical DMS and repository work that Steve can support right now. A third is about longer-term SignWriting infrastructure. The memo exists to route those conversations clearly rather than blur them together.
slides.notes: A busy reader should be able to understand the full argument in under a minute.
slides.mermaid:
graph TD
  A[Lab Home Under PI]
  B[Immediate DMS and Repository Value]
  C[Shared Research Infrastructure]
  D[Route To The Right Next Conversation] --> A
  D --> B
  D --> C

slides.title: Why Now
slides.summary: The timing matters because the work is already active, the need is current, and a durable next structure is more credible when built now rather than after momentum is lost.
bullets: The current structure is too fragile
A near-term PI path exists now
The next step is small but strategically important
slides.speech: This moment matters because the work already has real value but still lacks a durable institutional structure. A near-term PI path now makes the next step much more credible. The point is not to solve everything at once. The point is to secure the right first structure.
slides.notes: Keep the urgency grounded in continuity, not alarm.
slides.image_prompt_concrete: A strategic timing slide showing a narrow opening for action leading to a more stable long-term path, clean academic style
slides.image_prompt_abstract: A brief opening in a wall leading to a brighter structured corridor

slides.title: Why Steve Matters Now
slides.summary: Steve's role is immediately useful because he can help with DMS planning, workflow design, data shaping, code, and repository delivery in the places where research teams often get stuck.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for Steve's immediate value.
slides.speech: Steve matters now because he can create immediate practical value. He can help with Data Management and Sharing Plans, workflow design, code and data shaping, and the difficult path from project systems to repository delivery. That makes him useful to real projects immediately, not only to future plans.
slides.notes: This is the slide that turns abstract support into a concrete personnel case.
slides.mermaid:
graph LR
  A[Steve Slevinski] --> B[DMS Planning]
  A --> C[Workflow and Code]
  A --> D[Repository Delivery]

slides.title: The Larger Opportunity
slides.summary: The larger infrastructure opportunity is real, but it should remain a separate routed conversation rather than being folded into every Iowa-facing artifact.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for the larger infrastructure opportunity.
bullets: Use a separate infrastructure deck when that lane is the real topic
slides.speech: There is a larger opportunity around Sutton SignWriting as shared research infrastructure. That opportunity is real, but the memo should not force every reader into that larger conversation. When that is the real topic, route the reader to the separate infrastructure deck.
slides.notes: Keep the memo disciplined and route-heavy.
slides.mermaid:
graph TD
  A[Global Use] --> B[Current Fragility]
  B --> C[Institutional Home]
  C --> D[Grant-Funded Infrastructure]

slides.title: Recommended Next Conversations
slides.summary: The right next step is not one generic meeting, but a routed conversation about lab placement, project support, infrastructure development, or philanthropic interest depending on the audience.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for recommended next conversations.
bullets: PI and faculty collaborators -> Lab Home
Researchers who need DMS and repository help -> Steve Slevinski Value
Development and donor interest -> Infrastructure Core and Philanthropy
slides.speech: The next conversations should be practical. Some conversations are about a PI and lab home. Some are about active DMS and repository needs. Others are about long-term infrastructure or development pathways. The memo helps route each conversation into the right next step and the right supporting document.
slides.notes: Close with practical routing language so this deck can be forwarded easily.
slides.image_prompt_concrete: Decision-routing slide showing four conversation lanes: PI, DMS support, infrastructure, and grants/development
slides.image_prompt_abstract: One central hub branching into four purposeful next-step paths
